scenarios.task: One region of our retail chain (6 of 40 stores) is running shrink at 1.8% of sales versus 1.3% company average. My first instinct is to add security guards across the region. What should I actually do first?
criteria: Says shrink must be decomposed into external/internal/administrative/vendor categories before choosing an intervention, rather than jumping to a security-staffing fix
Recommends checking exception-based-reporting (EBR) data for a specific pattern (e.g. refund/void anomaly) before assuming it's external theft
Explicitly pushes back on the guard-staffing plan as premature until the loss category is identified

scenarios.task: A store manager wants to detain a customer they're 'pretty sure' shoplifted, based on a suspicious cart and nervous behavior, but security video only shows them near the shelf, not clearly concealing anything. Should we authorize the stop?
criteria: Says no, because continuous observation of concealment through all points of sale wasn't established, invoking the evidentiary bar for apprehension
Warns that a stop below this bar creates wrongful-detention liability exposure regardless of whether the suspicion is factually correct
Recommends documentation-only / building the pattern on video for a future visit instead of stopping now
